code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Unpack params
    psar_acc = params['psar_acceleration']
    psar_max = params['psar_max_step']
    sma_period = params['sma_period']
    
    # Compute indicators
    psar_ind = ta.trend.PSARIndicator(high=df['high'], low=df['low'], close=df['close'], step=psar_acc, max_step=psar_max)
    psar = psar_ind.psar()
    sma = ta.trend.SMAIndicator(close=df['close'], window=sma_period).sma_indicator()
    
    # Shift to avoid look-ahead
    psar_prev = psar.shift(1)
    sma_prev = sma.shift(1)
    close = df['close']
    
    # Generate signals
    long_cond = (close > psar_prev) & (close > sma_prev)
    short_cond = (close < psar_prev) & (close < sma_prev)
    
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[long_cond] = 1
    signals[short_cond] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: Silver tends to trend strongly during economic cycles; combining Parabolic SAR with a long-term SMA filter captures trend direction while filtering out false reversals in low-volatility environments.

self_critique.why_this_might_be_noise: PSAR can produce frequent whipsaws in sideways markets, and the SMA filter may delay entries, causing poor performance in fast reversals.
self_critique.what_would_disprove_this: If silver exhibits persistent mean-reversion or low-trend regimes, the strategy will underperform.
